publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: Son of Reinhard Oppel and his fourth wife, Elfriede Oppel (née Dey); seventh child of Reinhard Oppel.

Kurt and his younger sister, Brigitte, are mentioned several times in the correspondence between Reinhard and Jeanette Schenker, e.g. “The children are well. Bepo and Brigitte are developing magnificently; they are my entire joy in the daily struggles of life” (OJ 13/10, [192], March 3, 1935). Reinhard took Kurt several times to his homeland of Oberhessen. Throughout the correspondence he is referred to as “Bepo.”

Three photographs survive: OJ 72/11, item 4a, showing Brigitte and Kurt side-by-side, inscribed on the reverse: “Summer 1938”; OJ 72/11, item 4b with Kurt and Brigitte standing among flowers, inscribed: “June 12, 1935 / Bepo 4½, Brigitte 3 years old”; and OJ 72/11, item 7, showing Reinhard with Kurt and Brigitte standing at his sides, dated “Summer 1938.”

Kurt Oppel was instrumental in providing Timothy L. Jackson with a large cache of documents that had been concealed during World War II and retrieved by him in 1990, comprising correspondences with Reinhard Oppel’s colleagues, friends, and others, compositions, analytical and other work done for Heinrich Schenker, and much other handwritten and printed material, which is now preserved in the Oppel Collection in the Willis Library of the University of North Texas at Denton, USA.

bibl.ref: Reinhard Oppel Collection in UNT Digital Library. University of North Texas Libraries
UNT Music Library: Reinhard Oppel Collection
